findings.claim: The fictional resale platform is named 'Rundgång', which is Swedish for 'circulation/round' — the exact English word keyed at Gap (1) ('circulation').
findings.evidence: Passage: 'the resale platform Rundgång'; Gap (1) key = C 'circulation'. Audience is Swedish HP test-takers (ELF). A bilingual student who translates the brand name gets a thematic nudge toward 'circulation'.
findings.why: Not verdict-refuting: the gap is still resolved by English collocation ('keep the value in circulation' vs non-idiomatic 'in rotation/distribution/suspension'), and the Swedish meaning does not discriminate among the three circulatory-flavoured distractors. It is a real, minor observation none of the three stages recorded, worth a note rather than a rejection.
